zh.Ditton Priors, Shropshire, England: 英國史洛普爾迪頓 普利奧斯
zh.Arundel Castle, Sussex, England: 英國素可阿盧德爾 卡素
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John FitzAlan於大約1351年出生在英國素可阿盧德爾時，他的父親Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel46歲，母親Eleanor of Lancaster34歲。他與Eleanor Mautravers結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1379年12月16日在亞特 澤亞享年29歲，埋葬在英國素可列維斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John FitzAlan于大约1351年出生在英国素可阿盧德爾时，他的父亲Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel46岁，母亲Eleanor of Lancaster34岁。他与Eleanor Mautravers结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1379年12月16日在亞特 澤亞享年29岁，埋葬在英国素可列維斯。

nameMeanings.name: FitzAlan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Norman, English: relationship name from Anglo-Norman French fiz ‘son’ ( see Fitz ) + the personal name Alan ( see Allen ), ‘son of Alan’.

variants.name: Fitz
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Allen
meanings: English and Scottish: from the Middle English, Old French personal name Alain, Alein (Old Breton Alan), from a Celtic personal name of great antiquity and obscurity. In England the personal name is now usually spelled Alan, the surname Allen; in Scotland the surname is more often Allan. From 1139 it was common in Scotland, where the surname also derives from Gaelic Ailéne, Ailín, from ail ‘rock’. The present-day frequency of the surname Allen in England and Ireland is partly accounted for by the popularity of the personal name among Breton followers of William the Conqueror, by whom it was imported first to Britain and then to Ireland. Saint Alan(us) was a 5th-century bishop of Quimper, who was a cult figure in medieval Brittany. Another Saint Al(l)an was a Cornish or Breton saint of the 6th century, to whom a church in Cornwall is dedicated.
English: occasionally perhaps from the rare Middle English femaje personal name Aline (Old French Adaline, Aaline), a pet form of ancient Germanic names in Adal-, especially Adalheidis (see Allis ).
French: variant of   Allain  , a cognate of 1 above, and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John FitzAlan was born about 1351, in Arundel, Sussex, England, his father, Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel, was 46 and his mother, Eleanor of Lancaster, was 34. He married Eleanor Mautravers. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 16 December 1379, in At Sea, at the age of 29, and was buried in Lewes, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John FitzAlan est né environ 1351, à Arundel, Sussex, Angleterre, son père, Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Eleanor of Lancaster, avait 34 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor Mautravers. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 16 décembre 1379, à En mer, à l’âge de 29 ans, et a été enterré à Lewes, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John FitzAlan um 1351 in Arundel, Sussex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel 46 und seine Mutter Eleanor of Lancaster 34. Er hat Eleanor Mautravers geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 16. Dezember 1379 im Alter von 29 Jahren in Auf dem Meer und wurde in Lewes, Sussex, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John FitzAlan è nato il circa 1351 nel Arundel, Sussex, England, il suo padre, Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel, aveva 46 anni e il suo madre, Eleanor of Lancaster, aveva 34 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor Mautravers. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 16 dicembre 1379, nel At Sea, all'età di 29 anni, e fu sepolto a Lewes, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John FitzAlanが約1351年にイギリススセックスアルンデルで生まれた時，父Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundelは46歳，母Eleanor of Lancasterは34歳であった。Eleanor Mautraversと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1379年12月16日にアト セアで29歳で死去し，イギリススセックスルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John FitzAlan(이)가 대략 1351년 영국 수세스 아런들에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Eleanor of Lancaster(은)는 34세였다. 그는 Eleanor Mautravers(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1379년 12월 16일 앳 시에서 29세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 수세스 루스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John FitzAlan nasceu aproximadamente 1351, em Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Eleanor of Lancaster, tinha 34 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor Mautravers. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 16 de dezembro de 1379, em Em alto mar, com 29 anos, e foi sepultado em Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John FitzAlan родился около 1351 г. в местности арундель, суссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Eleanor of Lancaster, 34. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor Mautravers. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 16 декабря 1379 г. в местности ат сеа в возрасте 29 лет и был похоронен в местности львесь, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John FitzAlan nació aproximadamente 1351, en Arundel, Sussex, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Richard FitzAlan 10th Earl of Arundel, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Eleanor of Lancaster, tenía 34 años. Se casó con Eleanor Mautravers. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de diciembre de 1379, en En el mar, a la edad de 29 años, y fue sepultado en Lewes, Sussex, Inglaterra.
